claim_boundary: finite observer-local theorem candidate under declared SU(2) coherent-patch scaling; not a continuum de Sitter theorem, dS/CFT construction, or ER=EPR proof

major_claims.claim: For fixed excitation cutoff R and L > 2R, the observer-local tangent-plane operators obey [a_L,a_L^*] >= (1 - 2R/L) I on the low-excitation window.
For fixed R, the tangent-plane commutator lower bounds increase and the local strong-continuity bounds decrease along the audited cutoff family.

The tangent-plane candidate passes the finite anti-tautology, CP/trace refinement, covariance, screen-shadow, norm-faithfulness, commutator, and strong-continuity gates, leaving only Type-II/static-patch interpretation conditional.
The local low-excitation matrix block is norm-faithful under the observer-local isometric refinement.
Matrix-valued screen fibers and modular crossed-product shifts remain conditional because their physical origin is not derived from scalar screen data alone.

expert_question: Is the coherent-state tangent-plane sector the right finite static-patch analogue of the observer-local algebra, or should the noncommutative subsystem instead come from an edge-mode fiber or modular crossed product?
